examples.title: FIFO Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of First In First Out page replacement algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FIFO_Page_Replacement
INPUT: Reference string, Number of frames
OUTPUT: Number of page faults
BEGIN
  frames ← empty queue
  pageFaults ← 0
  
  FOR each page in reference string DO
    IF page NOT IN frames THEN
      IF frames is full THEN
        // Remove oldest page (front of queue)
        oldest ← frames.dequeue()
        remove oldest from frames
      END IF
      // Add new page to queue
      frames.enqueue(page)
      pageFaults ← pageFaults + 1
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  RETURN pageFaults
END
examples.explanation: FIFO maintains pages in arrival order. When a new page arrives and all frames are full, the oldest page (first to arrive) is removed. This is simple but may not always give optimal results.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is number of frames

examples.title: LRU Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of Least Recently Used page replacement using counter
examples.code:
def LRU_Page_Replacement(pages, frames):
    # Initialize frame array with -1 (empty)
    frame_array = [-1] * frames
    # Counter for time since last reference
    last_used = [0] * frames
    time = 0
    page_faults = 0
    
    for page in pages:
        time += 1
        # Check if page is already in frame
        if page in frame_array:
            # Update last used time for that frame
            idx = frame_array.index(page)
            last_used[idx] = time
            continue
        
        # Page fault occurs
        page_faults += 1
        
        # Find empty frame if available
        if -1 in frame_array:
            idx = frame_array.index(-1)
            frame_array[idx] = page
            last_used[idx] = time
        else:
            # Replace LRU page (minimum last_used value)
            lru_idx = last_used.index(min(last_used))
            frame_array[lru_idx] = page
            last_used[lru_idx] = time
    
    return page_faults

# Example usage
pages = [7, 0, 1, 2, 0, 3, 0, 4, 2, 3, 0, 3, 2]
frames = 3
print(f"Page Faults: {LRU_Page_Replacement(pages, frames)}")
examples.explanation: LRU replaces the page that has not been used for the longest time. It maintains a counter for each frame updated on each page hit. When replacement is needed, the frame with the smallest counter (oldest use time) is selected.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n * f) where n is pages and f is frames

examples.title: Contiguous Memory Allocation - First Fit
examples.description: C implementation of first-fit memory allocation strategy
examples.explanation: First-fit allocation scans memory from the beginning and allocates the first block that is large enough for the request. It is fast but may lead to external fragmentation over time.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of memory blocks

examples.title: Demand Paging Page Fault Handler
examples.description: Algorithm showing steps when handling a page fault in demand paging system
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Handle_Page_Fault
INPUT: virtual_address that caused fault
BEGIN
  // Step 1: Identify the faulting virtual page
  virtual_page_number ← virtual_address / PAGE_SIZE
  
  // Step 2: Check if page is valid (not in valid address space)
  IF NOT is_valid_page(virtual_page_number) THEN
    terminate_process()
    RETURN
  END IF
  
  // Step 3: Find a free frame in physical memory
  IF free_frames_available() THEN
    frame_number ← allocate_free_frame()
  ELSE
    // Step 4: Apply page replacement algorithm
    victim_page ← select_victim_page()
    frame_number ← victim_page.frame
    
    // If victim page is dirty, write to disk
    IF victim_page.dirty THEN
      write_to_disk(victim_page, swap_space)
    END IF
  END IF
  
  // Step 5: Load the required page from disk to frame
  page_location ← locate_page_on_disk(virtual_page_number)
  read_from_disk(page_location, frame_number)
  
  // Step 6: Update page table entry
  page_table[virtual_page_number].valid ← true
  page_table[virtual_page_number].frame ← frame_number
  page_table[virtual_page_number].dirty ← false
  
  // Step 7: Restart the interrupted instruction
  restart_instruction()
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm shows the complete page fault handling process in demand paging. The OS must locate the page on disk, possibly replace an existing page, update page tables, and restart the process instruction.
